description_long: This is a stone memorial obelisk set against a graffiti-covered wall, featuring a rectangular plaque with a French inscription. The obelisk has a classic, upright rectangular form with a slightly peaked top and a prominent, tiered base. The front face is smooth and bears the inscription, while the sides and back are plainer, showing some weathering and discoloration. The base is broad and stepped, providing stability and a sense of monumentality. The stone surface is rough and matte, with visible signs of age and exposure to the elements. The overall appearance is solemn and commemorative, typical of urban memorials or grave markers.
description: A weathered stone memorial obelisk with a French inscription, set on a broad, tiered base and backed by a graffiti-marked wall. The surface is rough and matte, with visible signs of age.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thickness of the obelisk and the depth of the base, with the inscription edge barely visible.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the peaked top and the full inscription, as well as the graffiti on the wall behind.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the depth and structure of the base and the upright slab.
description_view_3: Frontal view displaying the full French inscription, the peaked top, and the broad, stepped base against a graffiti-covered wall.
attribute_inscription: A large, centered French inscription is engraved on the front face of the obelisk, in uppercase serif letters. The text is legible and commemorative, referencing Philibert Aspairt and specific dates.
attribute_ornamentation: None visible; the obelisk is plain except for the inscription.
attribute_base_design: The base is broad, rectangular, and tiered, with two visible steps leading up to the main body. It is made of the same stone material and is unadorned.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is slightly peaked, forming a shallow triangular pediment above the inscription, with no additional decoration.
attribute_surrounding_features: A graffiti-covered wall is immediately behind the obelisk, providing a contrasting urban backdrop. No other features are visible.
